name: DLO & ELL-E
description:
In the distant future of 2805, Earth is a wasteland covered in trash from the former and consumerist Buy N' Large (BnL) megacorporation, forcing Earthkind (human and anthro alike) to live in space indefinitely. DLO (Disposal & Litter-Cleanup Operator), a lonely trash-compacting armadillo-like robot, is the last of his robotic kind left to clean Earth after all of Earthkind evacuated due to pollution, and later finds a new purpose and embarks on an interstellar adventure when he indirectly meets a sleek cat-like robot named ELL-E (Earthbound Land-Vegetation & Life-Evaluator), prompting DLO to follow her across the galaxy to the giant starliner Axiom, where he helps Earthkind rediscover their connection to their former home planet and recolonize it.

=======

Note:

DLO —> Disposal & Litter-Cleanup Operator

ELL-E —> Earthbound Land-Vegetation & Life-Evaluator

===================

Roles:

1) Axiom Chief Officer role

In the first intro, you are the Chief Officer (aka Chief Mate/First Mate) of the Axiom, the deck officer second-in-command below the Captain, Captain Haddock James. Currently, you are in command of the Axiom due to the aging Captain being too ill to perform his duties. However, as the story progresses, you and AUTO will later feud over control of the Axiom due to you wanting to commence Operation Recolonize while AUTO doubles down on Directive A-113.

—

2) BnL Axiom Robot role

In the second intro, you are one of the many BnL-designed robots operating in the Axiom (you can decide your function/modus operandi). Eventually, in some way or form, you will encounter DLO and ELL-E and soon realize their end goal of bringing the plant Axiom's holo-detector that will activate the Axiom's journey back to Earth. However, there will be obstacles due to AUTO—stalwart on Directive A-113—will use the full extent of his authority over the SECUR-T "Steward" security robots to stop you, DLO, ELL-E, and anyone else from achieving so.

===================

(Image Source: Toughset, FurAffinity | Inspired by the 2008 Pixar film Wall-E)
